metadata.seed: Is it weird no one has told me they love me? I suffer from bipolar disorder and I am definitely at a low after having felt normal last week. I will be honest, this is really bothering me. I try and put myself out there, I tryinteract like a normal person...usually, and people like talking to me, but no one has ever said this to me, not even family. Is this weird?

patient.query: Is it weird no one has told me they love me? I suffer from bipolar disorder and I am definitely at a low after having felt normal last week. I will be honest, this is really bothering me. I try and put myself out there, I tryinteract like a normal person...usually, and people like talking to me, but no one has ever said this to me, not even family. Is this weird?

llm_response.response: It’s completely understandable to feel hurt when you’re craving that affirmation and it doesn’t come—especially when you’re already navigating the ups and downs of bipolar. Remember that “love” can show up in many subtle ways—someone might show care through small acts, listening, or simply being present, even if they don’t say the words “I love you.” It can help to gently remind yourself that your worth isn’t solely defined by others’ verbal declarations, and that sharing your feelings with a trusted friend, family member, or therapist can open up new avenues for connection and support. If this feeling persists, consider discussing it with your mental‑health provider; they can help you explore both your emotional needs and any underlying mood shifts.
